lines.en_lit: MISHNA: the children, one does not afflict them on Yom Kippur; however, one trains them one year before or two years before so that they will be accustomed to mitzvot.
GEMARA: Since two before necessary to say that one trains them one year before? Rav Ḥisda said: not difficult. This to a feeble that to a healthy

Rav Huna said: One trains of eight and nine for hours; ten and eleven they complete by rabbinic at twelve they complete by Torah to girls And Rav Naḥman said: At nine ten one trains them for hours; at eleven and twelve they complete by rabbinic at thirteen they complete by Torah to boys. And Rabbi Yoḥanan said: There is no completing by rabbinic at ten and eleven one trains them for hours; and at twelve complete by Torah

We learned the children, one does not afflict them on Yom Kippur; however, one trains them one year before or two before Granted, Rav Huna and Rav Naḥman, one year before or two before year before by rabbinic and two before by rabbinic
But to Rabbi Yoḥanan, difficult. Rabbi Yoḥanan said to you: What one year or two preceding their maturity.

Come hear that Rabba bar Shmuel taught children, one does not afflict them on Yom Kippur; however, one trains them one year or two preceding their maturity. Granted, to Rabbi Yoḥanan, this works out well; but to Rav Huna and Rav Naḥman, difficult. The Rabbis say to you: What is training that is also taught a complete

And is training called completion? Wasn’t taught What is training? If was accustomed to eat at the second one feeds him at the third at the third one feeds him at the fourth Rava bar Ulla said: these are two training.

MISHNA: a pregnant woman who smelled one feeds her until she recovers, ill one feeds him according to experts And if there are no experts there, one feeds him according to his own until he says enough

GEMARA: The Sages taught a pregnant woman who smelled consecrated meat or pig meat one inserts a thin reed into the juice and places it on her mouth. If her mind become settled it is well. And if not, one feeds her the gravy itself If her mind becomes settled it is well. And if not, one feeds her the fat itself, as there is no that stands in the way of saving a life except for idol worship, and forbidden sexual relationships, and bloodshed.

idol worship from where we As it was taught Rabbi Eliezer says: If it is stated: and with all your soul” why is it stated “And with all your might” And if it is stated: “With all your might,” why is it stated: “With all your soul”?

if there is a person whose body is more beloved to him than his property, therefore it is stated: “With all your soul.” And if there is a person whose property is more beloved to him than his body, therefore it is stated: “With all your might.”

forbidden sexual relations or shed blood from where we As it was taught Rabbi says: “For as when a man rises against his fellow and slays him, even so is this matter” What idea did we learn about a betrothed woman from a murderer?

Rather, this come to teach is found to subject of teaching. Just as the betrothed woman, given to save her at life of so too, murderer Just as murderer let him be killed, and let him not transgress, so too, a betrothed young woman. let him be killed, and let him not transgress,
